definition:
ὧδε, adv., 
 [in LXX for חֲלֹם ,פֹּה ,הִנֶּה, etc. ;] 
   1. prop., of manner, so (Hom., al.). 
   2. In poets (rarely) and late writers, of place, 
  (a) hither (BL, §25, 2): Mat.8:29, Mrk.11:3, Luk.9:41, Jhn.6:25, al.; ἕως ὧδε,  Luk.23:5; 
   (b) here: Mat.12:6, Mrk.9:1, Luk.9:33, Jhn.6:9, al.; τὰ ὧδε, Col.4:9; opposite to ἐκεῖ, Heb.7:8; ὧδε . . . ἢ ὧδε (ἐκεῖ), Mat.24:23, Mrk.13:21; metaphorically, here (i.e. in this circumstance or connection), 1Co.4:2, Rev.13:10, 18 14:12 17:9. 
 (AS)
